SimpleCoder: 
import random

def display_random_quote(file_path):
    try:
        with open(file_path, 'r') as file:
            quotes = file.readlines()
            print(random.choice(quotes).strip())
    except FileNotFoundError:
        print("The file was not found.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    display_random_quote("quotes.txt")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

import pytest
from unittest.mock import mock_open, patch
from SimpleCoder import display_random_quote

def test_display_random_quote_success():
    mock_quotes = "First quote\nSecond quote\nThird quote"
    with patch("builtins.open", mock_open(read_data=mock_quotes)):
        with patch("builtins.print") as mocked_print:
            display_random_quote("quotes.txt")
            mocked_print.assert_called()  # Ensures a quote is printed

def test_display_random_quote_file_not_found():
    with patch("builtins.open", side_effect=FileNotFoundError):
        with patch("builtins.print") as mocked_print:
            display_random_quote("nonexistent.txt")
            mocked_print.assert_called_once_with("The file was not found.")

def test_display_random_quote_empty_file():
    with patch("builtins.open", mock_open(read_data="")):
        with patch("builtins.print") as mocked_print:
            display_random_quote("empty.txt")
            mocked_print.assert_not_called()  # No quote to print

def test_display_random_quote_one_line():
    with patch("builtins.open", mock_open(read_data="Only one quote")):
        with patch("builtins.print") as mocked_print:
            display_random_quote("one_line.txt")
            mocked_print.assert_called_once_with("Only one quote")

def test_display_random_quote_multiple_calls():
    mock_quotes = "First quote\nSecond quote\nThird quote"
    with patch("builtins.open", mock_open(read_data=mock_quotes)):
        with patch("random.choice", side_effect=["First quote", "Second quote", "Third quote"]):
            with patch("builtins.print") as mocked_print:
                display_random_quote("quotes.txt")
                display_random_quote("quotes.txt")
                display_random_quote("quotes.txt")
                assert mocked_print.call_count == 3

import pytest
from unittest.mock import mock_open, patch
from SimpleCoder import display_random_quote

def test_display_random_quote_with_multiple_quotes():
    mock_quotes = "Quote one\nQuote two\nQuote three"
    with patch("builtins.open", mock_open(read_data=mock_quotes)):
        with patch("builtins.print") as mocked_print:
            display_random_quote("quotes.txt")
            assert mocked_print.call_count == 1

def test_display_random_quote_with_single_quote():
    mock_quote = "Only one quote in this file"
    with patch("builtins.open", mock_open(read_data=mock_quote)):
        with patch("builtins.print") as mocked_print:
            display_random_quote("single_quote.txt")
            mocked_print.assert_called_once_with("Only one quote in this file")

def test_display_random_quote_file_not_exist():
    with patch("builtins.open", side_effect=FileNotFoundError):
        with patch("builtins.print") as mocked_print:
            display_random_quote("does_not_exist.txt")
            mocked_print.assert_called_once_with("The file was not found.")

def test_display_random_quote_with_empty_quotes():
    mock_quotes = "\n\n"  # File with empty lines
    with patch("builtins.open", mock_open(read_data=mock_quotes)):
        with patch("builtins.print") as mocked_print:
            display_random_quote("empty_lines.txt")
            assert mocked_print.call_count == 1

def test_display_random_quote_no_repeated_quotes():
    mock_quotes = "Quote one\nQuote two\nQuote three"
    with patch("builtins.open", mock_open(read_data=mock_quotes)):
        with patch("random.choice", side_effect=["Quote one", "Quote two", "Quote three"]):
            with patch("builtins.print") as mocked_print:
                display_random_quote("quotes.txt")
                display_random_quote("quotes.txt")
                display_random_quote("quotes.txt")
                printed_quotes = [call.args[0] for call in mocked_print.mock_calls]
                assert len(set(printed_quotes)) == 3, "Expected three different quotes"
